description: The pipeline extracted a metadata value under the evaluated attribute, but the value is actually used in the BioSample record as another kind of attribute or experimental role. Prefer this category over selection categories when the upstream extraction is already wrong, even if selection also failed to reject the candidate. Examples include extracting a ChIP target, assay target, antibody target, transgene, or measured gene as knockout_gene, knockdown_gene, or overexpressed_gene; extracting a treatment reagent as disease; or extracting a disease mention as tissue.

The extracted value is mentioned in the BioSample metadata, but it does not represent the sample itself or the evaluated sample attribute. Examples include assay names, sequencing methods, antibody names, culture media, project labels, comparison groups, or other contextual entities. For cell_line, this includes cases where the metadata mentions a stem cell line or other source cell line before differentiation, but the actual sample is a differentiated cell derived from that cell line rather than the original cell line itself.
The extraction or interpretation over-resolved an abbreviation, acronym, or short symbol whose intended meaning is ambiguous or unsupported by the metadata.

The extraction used the wrong text boundary, such as taking only part of a composite expression or including extra words, causing the extracted value to mean something different from the BioSample metadata.
The extracted value is not directly supported by the BioSample metadata and appears to have been inferred or hallucinated from weak context.

The extraction is reasonable, but none of the available ontology candidates is appropriate or there is not enough evidence, and the selection step should have rejected all candidates instead of producing a final mapping.
The extraction is reasonable, but the selection step chose a candidate mainly because of label, synonym, or abbreviation similarity while ignoring the broader BioSample context.
The extraction preserved the intended phrase, but the selection step accepted an ontology term that corresponds to only part of that phrase and loses important meaning.
The extraction is reasonable, but the selected ontology term is too broad, too narrow, or otherwise at an inappropriate level of specificity for the BioSample metadata.
The pipeline produced no final ontology mapping even though the BioSample metadata appears to contain a mappable entity for the evaluated attribute.
The error appears to be caused primarily by a missing, obsolete, or version-mismatched ontology term rather than by extraction or selection behavior.

The BioSample metadata itself is ambiguous, contradictory, or too underspecified for reliable interpretation, even for a human reviewer. Examples include different plausible cell line names appearing in different metadata fields, such as source_name and cell line, without enough context to tell which one represents the sample itself.
The previous evaluator judgment is itself wrong: the final ontology mapping is actually appropriate for the BioSample metadata, even though it was judged false.
The error does not fit the other categories.
